IRS990.ActivityOrMissionDesc: INSPIRING AND ORGANIZING COMMUNITIES TO BUILD VOLUNTEER ORGANIZATIONS EMPOWERED TO PURSUE SOCIAL JUSTICE.
IRS990.MissionDesc: TO ENGAGE EXCLUSIVELY IN CHARITABLE, EDUCATIONAL, AND SOCIAL PURPOSES FOR THE BENEFIT OF LOW-INCOME PERSONS. TO CREATE A NETWORK OF ORGANIZED COMMUNITIES PURSUING SOCIAL JUSTICE THROUGH SYSTEMIC AND STRUCTURAL TRANSFORMATION.
IRS990.Desc: COMMUNITY ORGANIZING & CIVIC ENGAGEMENTIN 2024, DHF REACHED 18,200 VOTERS AND REGISTERED 925 NEW VOTERS THROUGH NON-PARTISAN CANVASSING AND VOTER DRIVES; LAUNCHED 5 NEW VECINOS UNIDOS CHAPTERS TO BUILD LOCAL, YEAR-ROUND POWER ACROSS THE CENTRAL VALLEY. WE ENGAGED 6,000 ATTENDEES AT COMMUNITY EVENTS, WHERE PERSONAL STORIES OF VOTING FOR THE FIRST TIME WERE SHARED, INSPIRING OTHERS TO MAKE THEIR VOICES HEARD.

ProgSrvcAccomActy2Grp.Desc: RESOURCE CENTERS & HEALTH ACCESS IN 2024, DHF HOSTED 4 MOBILE EYE CLINICS, PROVIDING 45 EYE EXAMS FOR FARMWORKERS AND ADMINISTERING 69 COVID AND FLU VACCINES. TO ADDRESS FOOD INSECURITY, THE FOUNDATION DISTRIBUTED 24 FOOD BOXES DURING THE HOLIDAY SEASON FROM THANKSGIVING TO CHRISTMAS. THROUGHOUT THE YEAR, DHF ALSO ATTENDED 50+ COMMUNITY EVENTS, SHARING VITAL RESOURCES AND INFORMATION WITH FAMILIES. STAFF SUPPORTED 1,260 UNDUPLICATED COMMUNITY MEMBERS WITH MEDICAL RE-ENROLLMENT, GUIDING THEM THROUGH THE ENROLLMENT PROCESS, AND OFFERED 500+ REFERRALS FOR URGENT NEEDS SUCH AS RESTORING ELECTRICITY, ACCESSING FOOD BOXES, AND RESOLVING ITIN QUESTIONS. THE FOUNDATION'S WORK WAS FURTHER STRENGTHENED BY 8 INTERNS, WHO CONTRIBUTED NEARLY 2,000 VOLUNTEER HOURS, EXPANDING DHF'S CAPACITY TO SERVE THE COMMUNITY.

ProgSrvcAccomActy3Grp.Desc: YOUTH LEADERSHIP & EDUCATION EQUITYIN 2024, DHF HELD 6 WORKSHOPS ON SCHOOL DISTRICT ACCOUNTABILITY, ENGAGING OVER 50 YOUTH AND VECINOS IN STRENGTHENING THEIR SKILLS AS ADVOCATES FOR EQUITY IN EDUCATION. THE FOUNDATION ALSO REACHED 847 STUDENTS THROUGH 119 EVENTS FOCUSED ON HIGHER EDUCATION AND CAREER READINESS, SUPPORTING YOUNG PEOPLE AS THEY EXPLORED PATHWAYS TO COLLEGE AND FUTURE CAREERS. BEYOND DIRECT PROGRAMMING, DHF YOUTH LEADERS HELPED SHAPE STATEWIDE CONVERSATIONS ON HEALTHIER SCHOOL MEALS AND FIRST-TIME VOTER ENGAGEMENT, WHILE THE EDUCATION EQUITY PROGRAM AND KERN EDUCATION JUSTICE COLLABORATIVE CONTINUED ORGANIZING FOR FAIR SCHOOL FUNDING AND RESTORATIVE JUSTICE PRACTICES. DHF ALSO CARRIED FORWARD LEGAL ACTION CHALLENGING THE APPROVAL OF A CHARTER SCHOOL THAT THREATENED STUDENTS WITH DISABILITIES AND ENGLISH LEARNERS, UNDERSCORING THE FOUNDATION'S COMMITMENT TO EDUCATIONAL EQUITY AND COMMUNITY VOICE.

Form990PartVIISectionAGrp.TitleTxt: DIRECTOR OF GOVT AND COMMUNITY RELATIONS

Form990ScheduleAPartVIGrp.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: SCHEDULE A, PART II, LINE 10, EXPLANATION OF OTHER INCOME:
Form990ScheduleAPartVIGrp.ExplanationTxt: FUNDRAISING INCOME - 2021 AMOUNT: $ 132,008. 2022 AMOUNT: $ 17,480. 2023 AMOUNT: $ 42,068. 2024 AMOUNT: $ 146,958.

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: DOLORES HUERTA FOUNDATION ARE RECOGNIZED AS TAX-EXEMPT ORGANIZATIONS UNDER SECTION 501(C)(3) OF THE INTERNAL REVENUE CODE AND THE CALIFORNIA REVENUE AND TAXATION CODE SECTION 23701D. AS SUCH, THE ORGANIZATION IS GENERALLY NOT SUBJECT TO FEDERAL OR STATE INCOME TAXES, AND NO PROVISION FOR INCOME TAXES HAS BEEN INCLUDED IN THE ACCOMPANYING FINANCIAL STATEMENTS. ADDITIONALLY, DOLORES HUERTA FOUNDATION IS CLASSIFIED AS A PUBLIC CHARITY UNDER IRC SECTIONS 509(A)(1) AND 170(B)(1)(A)(VI). HOWEVER, THE ORGANIZATION MAY BE SUBJECT TO INCOME TAX ON ANY NET INCOME EARNED FROM BUSINESS ACTIVITIES THAT ARE REGULARLY CARRIED OUT AND NOT DIRECTLY RELATED TO ITS EXEMPT PURPOSE. MANAGEMENT BELIEVES THAT ANY SUCH UNRELATED BUSINESS INCOME, IF IT EXISTS, IS NOT SIGNIFICANT ENOUGH TO IMPACT THE FINANCIAL STATEMENTS AS A WHOLE; AND, THEREFORE, NO TAX PROVISION HAS BEEN RECORDED. IN ACCORDANCE WITH THE FINANCIAL ACCOUNTING STANDARDS BOARD (FASB) ACCOUNTING STANDARDS CODIFICATION (ASC) TOPIC 740, UNCERTAINTY IN INCOME TAXES, THE ORGANIZATION RECOGNIZES TAX POSITIONS IN THE FINANCIAL STATEMENTS ONLY IF IT IS MORE LIKELY THAN NOT THAT THOSE POSITIONS WOULD BE UPHELD IN AN AUDIT, BASED ON THEIR TECHNICAL MERITS. MANAGEMENT HAS REVIEWED THE ORGANIZATION'S TAX POSITIONS, PARTICULARLY THE ONGOING QUALIFICATION FOR TAX-EXEMPT STATUS PURSUANT TO THE OPERATIONAL REQUIREMENTS UNDER IRC SECTION 501(C)(3) AND ANY POTENTIAL MATERIAL INCOME TAX LIABILITIES AND HAS CONCLUDED THAT ALL POSITIONS ARE LIKELY TO BE UPHELD IF AUDITED. AS A RESULT, NO DISCLOSURES RELATED TO UNCERTAIN TAX POSITIONS ARE REQUIRED. THE ORGANIZATION'S FEDERAL AND STATE INFORMATION RETURNS GENERALLY REMAIN SUBJECT TO EXAMINATION FOR THREE AND FOUR YEARS, RESPECTIVELY, AFTER THEY ARE FILED.
LAND OF DHF PEACE AND JUSTICE 978,935.

BusTrInvolveInterestedPrsnGrp.RelationshipDescriptionTxt: FAMILY MEMBER OF DOLORES HUERTA, AN OFFICER AND BOARD MEMBER.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: SCH L, PART IV, BUSINESS TRANSACTIONS INVOLVING INTEREST PERSONS:
FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION A, LINE 2
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: DOLORES HUERTA AND ALICIA ARONG ARE SISTERS. ANGELA CABRERA, FIDEL CABRERA, AND CAMILA CHAVEZ ARE SIBLINGS.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION A, LINE 4
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: DURING THE TAX YEAR, THE ORGANIZATION ADOPTED AMENDED AND RESTATED BYLAWS MATERIALLY AFFECTING GOVERNANCE. THE CHANGES INCLUDE: (I) ESTABLISHING A BOARD SIZE OF 13 VOTING DIRECTORS AND REVISING THE QUORUM TO A MAJORITY OF DIRECTORS IN OFFICE (MINIMUM 3); (II) RESTRUCTURING OFFICERS, CREATING BOARD OFFICERS (CHAIR, VICE CHAIR, SECRETARY, TREASURER) DISTINCT FROM CORPORATE OFFICERS (INCLUDING PRESIDENT AND EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR) AND DEFINING THEIR RESPECTIVE AUTHORITIES; (III) CLARIFYING DIRECTOR TERMS (3YEAR, STAGGERED; UNLIMITED CONSECUTIVE TERMS), REMOVAL, AND VACANCY PROCEDURES (INCLUDING ELECTRONIC WRITTEN CONSENTS/BOARD POLLS); (IV) UPDATING MEETING REQUIREMENTS (ANNUAL JANUARY MEETING; QUARTERLY MEETINGS WITH 10DAY NOTICE; SPECIAL MEETINGS WITH 48HOUR NOTICE; REMOTE PARTICIPATION; AGENDA/MINUTE PROCEDURES); (V) REVISING COMMITTEE PROVISIONS TO ADVISORY COMMITTEES ONLY AND ESTABLISHING AUDIT COMMITTEE REQUIREMENTS WHEN REVENUES ARE $2,000,000; (VI) REAFFIRMING THE 49% INTERESTED DIRECTOR LIMITATION AND ADOPTING ENHANCED CONFLICTOFINTEREST PROVISIONS; (VII) UPDATING FISCAL AUTHORITY (DUAL SIGNATURES AND A $5,000 CONTRACT THRESHOLD ABSENT BOARD APPROVAL); AND (VIII) ARTICULATING THE ORGANIZATION'S CHARITABLE PURPOSES/MISSION AND DISSOLUTION PROVISIONS CONSISTENT WITH IRC 501(C)(3).

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 11B
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: DRAFT COPIES OF FORM 990 WITH ALL STATEMENTS AND RELATED STATE REPORTS ARE PROVIDED TO THE BOARD AND THE ORGANIZATION'S ATTORNEY FOR REVIEW. BOARD MEMBERS ARE GIVEN TIME TO REVIEW, INQUIRE, AND RESOLVE ANY QUESTIONS AND REPLY WITH APPROVAL PRIOR TO FILING.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 12C
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY IS PROVIDED TO ALL EMPLOYEES AS PART OF THE BYLAW AND EMPLOYEE HAND BOOK AND IS MAINTAINED IN THE EMPLOYEE POLICY MANUAL. A COPY OF THE POLICY IS ALSO PROVIDED TO THE BOARD MEMBERS. THE ORGANIZATION HAS INSTITUTED AN ANNUAL QUESTIONAIRE FOR BOARD MEMBERS AND EMPLOYEES TO COMPLETE AND SIGN TO DISCLOSE ANY POTENTIAL CONFLICT OF INTEREST. IN CONNECTION WITH ANY ACTUAL OR POSSIBLE CONFLICT OF INTEREST, A DIRECTOR MUST DISCLOSE THE EXISTENCE OF ANT PERCEIVED OR ACTUAL CONFLICT OF INTEREST ASSOCATED WITH A PROPOSED ACTION UNDER CONSIDERATION BY THE BOD OR TO ANY ACTUAL CONFLICT OF INTEREST IN ACCORDANCE WITH THE BOARD'S CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY AND PROVIDE AND UPDATE ALL BOARD MEMBERS TO ENSYRE THEY ADHERE TO THEIR DUTY OF LOYALTY.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 15
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS HAS A PROCESS OF REVIEWING AND APPROVING THE COMPENSATION OF ITS EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR AND OTHER OFFICERS AND KEY EMPLOYEES, ON A REGULAR BASIS TO DETERMINE IT IS FAIR AND REASONABLE WITH THE GOAL OF RETAINING EMPLOYEES AT COMPENSATION LEVELS WITHIN APPROPRIATE MARKET RANGE. THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS SETS THE COMPENSATION PACKAGES OF THE EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR AND OTHER OFFICERS AND KEY EMPLOYEES BASED ON COMPARABLE MARKET DATA OF SIMILAR SIZED ORGANZATIONS AND INDUSTRY. THE COMPARABLE DATA INCLUDES THE 2022 COMPENSATION & BENEFITS SURVEY FOR SOUTHERN CALIFORNIA NONPROFITS. THE RESULTS OF THE SURVEY WERE PRESENTED TO THE FULL BOARD AND THE BOARD VOTED ON THE COMPENSATION PACKAGES, IN ADVANCE. THE ORGANZATION'S BOARD OF DIRECTORS CONTAMPORANEOUSLY DOCUMENTED ITS DELIBERATIONS AND DECISIONS REGARDING THE COMPENSATION ARRANGEMENTS, INCLUDING WHO WAS PRESENT, HOW THEY VOTED, AND A DESCRIPTION OF THE COMPARABILITY DATA AND HOW IT WAS OBTAINED.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION C, LINE 19
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE ORGANIZATION'S ANNUAL RETURN IS AVAILABLE BY WRITTEN REQUEST FROM THE OFFICES OF THE ORGANIZATION. IT IS ALSO AVAILABLE ONLINE THROUGH THE GUIDESTAR SYSTEM.
CONTRACT LABOR: PROGRAM SERVICE EXPENSES 455,370. MANAGEMENT AND GENERAL EXPENSES 283,172. FUNDRAISING EXPENSES 67,907. TOTAL EXPENSES 806,449.

BAD DEBT EXPENSES: PROGRAM SERVICE EXPENSES 0. MANAGEMENT AND GENERAL EXPENSES 36,000. FUNDRAISING EXPENSES 0. TOTAL EXPENSES 36,000. TAXES AND LICENSES: PROGRAM SERVICE EXPENSES 16,855. MANAGEMENT AND GENERAL EXPENSES 0. FUNDRAISING EXPENSES 3,996. TOTAL EXPENSES 20,851. SPONSORSHIPS AND GIFTS: PROGRAM SERVICE EXPENSES 0. MANAGEMENT AND GENERAL EXPENSES 0. FUNDRAISING EXPENSES 5,314. TOTAL EXPENSES 5,314. OTHER EXPENSES: PROGRAM SERVICE EXPENSES 0. MANAGEMENT AND GENERAL EXPENSES 1,431. FUNDRAISING EXPENSES 0. TOTAL EXPENSES 1,431.

IdRelatedTaxExemptOrgGrp.PrimaryActivitiesTxt: TO OPERATE EXCLUSIVELY TO ASSIST ON THE CHARITABLE PURPOSES OF DHF
TO EMPOWER AND ACTIVATE VOTERS FOR FAIR REPRESENTATION

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: SCHEDULE R, PART V, SECTION 2, ITEM (1)
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE $978,935 REPRESENTS THE PURCHASE OF LAND WHICH IS A PROGRAM EXPENSE OF THE DOLORES HUERTA FOUNDATION (DHF). DHF IS CONSTRUCTING ITS OWN PROGRAM FACILITY ON LAND HELD BY ITS IRC SECTION 509(A)(3) SUPPORTING ORGANIZATION, THE DHF PEACE AND JUSTICE CORPORATION. THE CAMPUS WILL: - SERVE AS THE HEADQUARTERS OF THE DHF; - HOUSE ROTATING ART INSTALLATIONS AND CULTURAL EXHIBITS; - PROVIDE A VIBRANT MULTICULTURAL COMMUNITY SPACE FOR ARTS, EVENTS, AND CIVIC ENGAGEMENT; AND - LAUNCH AN ORGANIZING INSTITUTE TO TRAIN GRASSROOTS LEADERS FROM ACROSS THE NATION. THE PROJECT ("THE DHF CAMPUS") WILL FURTHER DHF'S CHARITABLE AND EDUCATIONAL MISSION OF PROMOTING SOCIAL JUSTICE, COMMUNITY EMPOWERMENT, AND LEADERSHIP DEVELOPMENT. THE DHF PEACE AND JUSTICE CORPORATION HOLDS LEGAL TITLE TO THE PROPERTY SOLELY TO SUPPORT DHF'S CHARITABLE PURPOSES AND NOT FOR ITS INDEPENDENT USE OR FINANCIAL BENEFIT.
